title: thelypteris thelypterioides subsp. squamigera
intro: Thelypteris thelypterioides subsp. squamigera, also known as the squamigera fern, is a subspecies of the Thelypteris thelypterioides plant, a type of fern native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments such as fever, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The squamigera fern is valued for its health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves have also been used in herbal medicine to make ointments and poultices for topical application.
